html:
[807]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 暂忘 - Colloquial expression meaning 'temporarily forget' or 'for a moment forget.'
  • 明年至期 - Refers to the approaching next year or a specific upcoming time.
  • 果使 - A formal way of saying 'if indeed' or 'if it turns out that.'
  • 遂开封 - Means 'then opened' or 'then unsealed.'
  • 言词清丽 - Means 'words are clear and beautiful.'
  • 情意重叠 - Means 'emotions are layered' or 'feelings are complex.'
  • 书末有诗二首 - Means 'at the end of the letter, there are two poems.'
  • 河汉虽云濶三秋尚有期 - '河汉' refers to the Milky Way, and '三秋' means three autumns, symbolizing a long time. The phrase means 'Though the Milky Way is wide, there is still hope for three autumns.'
  • 情人终已矣良防更何时 - '情人' means 'lover,' '终已矣' means 'has ended,' and '良防更何时' means 'when will it be good again?' The phrase means 'The lover has ended, when will it be good again?'
  • 朱阁临清汉琼宫御紫房 - '朱阁' means 'red pavilion,' '清汉' means 'clear Han,' '琼宫' means 'jade palace,' and '紫房' means 'purple room.' The phrase describes a luxurious setting.
  • 佳期情在此只是断人膓 - '佳期' means 'good time' or 'auspicious moment,' '情在此' means 'the feeling is here,' and '只是断人膓' means 'it only breaks the heart.' The phrase means 'The good time is here, but it only breaks the heart.'
  • 翰以香牋答书意甚慊切 - '翰' refers to the scholar, '香牋' means 'fragrant paper,' '答书' means 'reply letter,' '意甚慊切' means 'the meaning is very earnest.' The phrase means 'The scholar replied with a fragrant letter, the meaning was very earnest.'
  • 并有酬赠诗二首 - Means 'and there were two poems in return.'
  • 人世将天上由来不可期 - '人世' means 'human world,' '天上' means 'heaven,' '由来' means 'originally,' '不可期' means 'cannot be expected.' The phrase means 'The human world and heaven cannot be expected from the beginning.'
  • 谁知一廻顾交作两相思 - '谁知' means 'who knows,' '一廻顾' means 'one look back,' '交作' means 'together,' '两相思' means 'two mutual loves.' The phrase means 'Who knows, one look back, and two mutual loves are created.'
  • 赠枕犹香泽啼衣尚泪防 - '赠枕' means 'gifted pillow,' '犹香泽' means 'still fragrant,' '啼衣' means 'crying clothes,' '尚泪防' means 'still tears.' The phrase means 'The gifted pillow is still fragrant, the crying clothes still have tears.'
  • 玉颜霄汉里空有往来魂 - '玉颜' means 'beautiful face,' '霄汉' means 'heaven,' '空有' means 'only have,' '往来魂' means 'soul coming and going.' The phrase means 'The beautiful face in heaven, only has a soul coming and going.'
  • 自此而绝是年太史奏织女星无光 - '自此而绝' means 'from this point on, it is cut off,' '是年' means 'that year,' '太史' means 'astronomer,' '奏' means 'report,' '织女星' means 'Vega,' '无光' means 'no light.' The phrase means 'From this point on, it is cut off, that year the astronomer reported that Vega had no light.'
  • 翰思不已凡人间丽色不复措意 - '翰思不已' means 'the scholar thought endlessly,' '凡人间' means 'in the human world,' '丽色' means 'beautiful colors,' '不复措意' means 'no longer pay attention.' The phrase means 'The scholar thought endlessly, in the human world, no longer pays attention to beautiful colors.'
  • 复以继嗣大义湏婚强娶程氏女所不称意 - '复以' means 'again because,' '继嗣大义' means 'the great virtue of continuing the lineage,' '湏婚' means 'must marry,' '强娶' means 'forcibly marry,' '程氏女' means 'Ms. Cheng,' '所不称意' means 'not to his liking.' The phrase means 'Again because of the great virtue of continuing the lineage, he must forcibly marry Ms. Cheng, which is not to his liking.'
  • 复以无嗣遂成反目翰后官至侍御史而卒 - '复以' means 'again because,' '无嗣' means 'no heir,' '遂成反目' means 'thus became estranged,' '翰后' means 'after the scholar,' '官至' means 'reached the rank of,' '侍御史' means 'censor,' '而卒' means 'and died.' The phrase means 'Again because of no heir, thus became estranged, after the scholar, he reached the rank of censor and died.'
  • 出灵怪集 - '出' means 'from,' '灵怪集' means 'Collection of Ghostly and Strange Stories.' The phrase means 'From the Collection of Ghostly and Strange Stories.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 使女 - Refers to a female servant or messenger.
  • 书函 - Refers to a letter or document.
  • 青缣 - Refers to a type of fine silk.
  • 铅丹 - Refers to red lead, a pigment used in writing.
  • 河汉 - Refers to the Milky Way.
  • 朱阁 - Refers to a red pavilion, a luxurious building.
  • 琼宫 - Refers to a jade palace, a luxurious building.
  • 紫房 - Refers to a purple room, a luxurious building.
  • 太史 - Refers to an astronomer or historian.
  • 织女星 - Refers to Vega, a star in the constellation Lyra.
  • 灵怪集 - Refers to a collection of ghostly and strange stories, a literary work.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sentence '暂忘明年至期果使前者使女书函致翰遂开封以青缣为铅丹为字言词清丽情意重叠书末有诗二首' must be read as a sequence of events: 'Temporarily forget, the approaching next year, if indeed the previous messenger girl delivered a letter to the scholar, then unsealed, with fine silk as the envelope and red lead as the writing, words are clear and beautiful, emotions are layered, at the end of the letter, there are two poems.'
  • The sentence '河汉虽云濶三秋尚有期情人终已矣良防更何时' is a compound structure: 'Though the Milky Way is wide, there is still hope for three autumns, the lover has ended, when will it be good again?'
  • The sentence '朱阁临清汉琼宫御紫房佳期情在此只是断人膓' is a compound structure: 'The red pavilion is near the clear Han, the jade palace governs the purple room, the good time is here, but it only breaks the heart.'
  • The sentence '翰以香牋答书意甚慊切并有酬赠诗二首' is a compound structure: 'The scholar replied with a fragrant letter, the meaning was very earnest, and there were two poems in return.'
  • The sentence '人世将天上由来不可期谁知一廻顾交作两相思' is a compound structure: 'The human world and heaven cannot be expected from the beginning, who knows, one look back, and two mutual loves are created.'
  • The sentence '赠枕犹香泽啼衣尚泪防玉颜霄汉里空有往来魂' is a compound structure: 'The gifted pillow is still fragrant, the crying clothes still have tears, the beautiful face in heaven, only has a soul coming and going.'

[808]

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 虢州 - A prefecture in the Tang Dynasty, located in present-day Henan Province.
  • 阌乡县 - A county in Guozhou Prefecture.
  • 天仙村 - A village in Changshou Township.
  • 元和十二年 - The 12th year of the Yuanhe era (817 AD) during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 天乐 - Heavenly music, often associated with divine or supernatural phenomena.
  • 村吏 - The village official, responsible for local administration.

Proper Nouns

  • 杨敬真 - A female given name.
  • 王清 - A male given name.

[809]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 遣吏民逺近寻逐 - Send officials and commoners to search far and wide; common in classical texts indicating a thorough search.
  • 衣閉其户以棘环之 - Close the door and surround it with thorns; symbolic action often used in classical texts to indicate sealing or protecting something.
  • 兾其或来也 - Hoping that they might come back; the word '兾' (冀) means 'to hope' and is often used in classical texts to express a wish.
  • 云中仙乐异香 - Divine music and strange fragrance in the clouds; poetic description of supernatural or divine phenomena.
  • 下王氏宅作乐 - Descend to Wang's house to make music; metaphorical way of saying that the divine beings visited Wang's house.
  • 面目光芒有非常之色 - Her face had a radiant glow and an extraordinary appearance; poetic description of a person's appearance, often used to describe divine or supernatural beings.
  • 准籍合仙 - According to the records, she is destined to become a fairy; reference to the belief in predestined fates or destinies in classical Chinese literature.
  • 使者来迎将防扵西岳 - Masters come to escort her to the Western Mountain; reference to the Western Mountain, which is often associated with immortality and the afterlife in Chinese mythology.
  • 珍华香洁 - Precious, fragrant, and clean; poetic description of something valuable and pure.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 县令李邯 - County magistrate Li Han; reference to a specific official in a historical context.
  • 王氏 - Wang family; reference to a specific family, likely a prominent one in the context of the story.
  • 仙骑 - Divine riders; reference to supernatural beings, often associated with immortality and the afterlife in Chinese mythology.
  • 西岳 - Western Mountain; reference to a specific mountain, often associated with immortality and the afterlife in Chinese mythology.
  • 玉箱 - Jade box; reference to a valuable container, often used in classical texts to symbolize precious items or documents.
  • 竒服 - Strange clothes; reference to unusual or supernatural attire, often associated with divine or immortal beings in Chinese mythology.

Proper Nouns

  • 县令李邯 - County magistrate Li Han.
  • 王氏 - Wang family.
  • 仙骑 - Divine riders.
  • 西岳 - Western Mountain.
  • 玉箱 - Jade box.
  • 竒服 - Strange clothes.

[810]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 名状遂衣之 - Compound phrase where '名状' refers to the act of naming or describing, and '遂衣之' means 'then dressed her.' Suggests a ritual or ceremonial act of naming and dressing.
  • 畢樂作三阕 - '畢樂' refers to a grand musical performance, and '作三阕' means 'composed three sections' or 'performed three parts.' Likely describes a musical or poetic performance.
  • 青衣引白 - '青衣' refers to a servant or attendant dressed in green, and '引白' means 'led the white jade (or white object).' Likely describes a ceremonial or ritualistic procession.
  • 宜乗此初尚惧其危 - '宜乗此初' means 'it is appropriate to ride at the beginning,' and '尚惧其危' means 'still fearing its danger.' Suggests a cautious approach to something new or risky.
  • 稳不可言 - '稳' means 'stable' or 'secure,' and '不可言' means 'cannot be described' or 'beyond words.' Suggests an experience that is so secure or profound that it defies description.
  • 飞起而五云捧出 - '飞起' means 'to fly up,' and '五云捧出' means 'five clouds supporting her out.' Likely describes a magical or supernatural ascent.
  • 防仗前引至扵华山云防峰 - '防仗' refers to a ceremonial guard or escort, and '前引至扵华山云防峰' means 'led to the peak of Mount Hua in the cloud barrier.' Describes a grand procession to a specific location.
  • 峰上有盘石已有四女先在彼焉 - '盘石' means 'flat stone,' and '四女先在彼焉' means 'four women were already there.' Suggests a gathering of women on a stone in the mountain.
  • 一人云姓马宋州人 - '一人云' means 'one said,' and '姓马宋州人' means 'her surname is Ma, from Songzhou.' Introduces the first woman's identity.
  • 一人姓徐幽州人 - '一人姓徐幽州人' means 'her surname is Xu, from Youzhou.' Introduces the second woman's identity.
  • 一人姓郭荆州人 - '一人姓郭荆州人' means 'her surname is Guo, from Jingzhou.' Introduces the third woman's identity.
  • 一人姓夏青州人 - '一人姓夏青州人' means 'her surname is Xia, from Qingzhou.' Introduces the fourth woman's identity.
  • 皆其夜成仙同防扵此 - '皆其夜成仙' means 'all became immortals that night,' and '同防扵此' means 'gathered here together.' Suggests a miraculous event where the women became immortals and gathered.
  • 旁一小仙曰并舍虚幻得证真仙 - '旁一小仙' means 'a small fairy on the side,' and '并舍虚幻得证真仙' means 'by abandoning illusion and attaining true immortality.' Suggests a commentary on the process of becoming an immortal.
  • 今当定名宜有真字扵是 - '今当定名' means 'now it is time to name them,' and '宜有真字扵是' means 'it is appropriate to have the character 'true' in their names.' Suggests a ritual of naming the immortals.
  • 信真诗曰 - '信真诗曰' means 'the poem of Xin Zhen says,' introducing the first poem.
  • 防刼澄烦虑 - '防刼' means 'to prevent the劫,' a Buddhist term for calamity or disaster, and '澄烦虑' means 'to clear away troubled thoughts.' Suggests a theme of overcoming obstacles and finding peace.
  • 思今身仅成 - '思今身仅成' means 'thinking that my body has just been formed,' suggesting a reflection on the recent transformation.
  • 誓将云外隐 - '誓将云外隐' means 'swearing to hide in the clouds,' suggesting a desire for seclusion and transcendence.
  • 不向世间存 - '不向世间存' means 'not to exist in the world,' suggesting a rejection of worldly life.
  • 湛真诗曰 - '湛真诗曰' means 'the poem of Zhan Zhen says,' introducing the second poem.
  • 绰约离尘世 - '绰约' means 'graceful,' and '离尘世' means 'to leave the world.' Suggests a graceful departure from worldly life.
  • 云衣无绽 - '云衣' means 'cloud robe,' and '无绽' means 'without seams.' Suggests a seamless and perfect garment, symbolizing purity and perfection.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 华山 - Refers to Mount Hua, one of the Five Great Mountains of China, known for its spiritual and cultural significance.
  • 云防峰 - Refers to a specific peak on Mount Hua, known for its mystical and spiritual associations.
  • 成仙 - Refers to the concept of becoming an immortal, a common theme in Chinese mythology and Daoist tradition.
  • 真仙 - Refers to true immortals, emphasizing the purity and authenticity of their state.
  • 防刼 - A Buddhist term for calamity or disaster, often used in Chinese literature to describe the challenges one must overcome to achieve enlightenment or immortality.

Proper Nouns

  • 马 - The surname of the first woman.
  • 宋州 - The place of origin of the first woman.
  • 徐 - The surname of the second woman.
  • 幽州 - The place of origin of the second woman.
  • 郭 - The surname of the third woman.
  • 荆州 - The place of origin of the third woman.
  • 夏 - The surname of the fourth woman.
  • 青州 - The place of origin of the fourth woman.
  • 华山 - The name of the mountain where the events take place.
  • 云防峰 - The name of the peak on Mount Hua.
  • 信真 - The name of the first immortal woman.
  • 湛真 - The name of the second immortal woman.

[811]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 日驾没遥程 - Poetic description, possibly referring to a journey or a metaphorical passage of time.
  • 既而雕盘珍果名不可知 - Describes a moment of awe or confusion, with 'exquisite fruits on a carved plate' and 'names unknown.'
  • 妙乐锵锽响动崖谷 - Describes a beautiful, resonant sound, with 'wonderful music' and 'resounding through the cliffs and valleys.'
  • 俄而执莭者曰宜往蓬莱谒大仙伯 - Describes a sudden command, with 'a person holding a staff' and 'it is advisable to go to Mount Penglai to meet the Great Immortal Lord.'
  • 五真曰大仙伯为谁 - Describes a question, with 'the Five Immortals' and 'who is the Great Immortal Lord.'
  • 曰茅君也 - Is a response, with 'Lord Mao, likely referring to a Daoist figure.'
  • 妓乐鸾复前引东去 - Describes a procession, with 'musicians and dancers' and 'phoenixes' leading the way.
  • 倐然间已到蓬莱 - Describes a sudden arrival, with 'suddenly' and 'already arrived at Mount Penglai.'
  • 其宫皆金银花木楼殿皆非人间之制作 - Describes the opulence of Mount Penglai, with 'gold and silver flowers and trees' and 'palaces and towers not made by human hands.'
  • 大仙伯居金阙玉堂中侍卫甚严 - Describes the residence of the Great Immortal Lord, with 'golden gate and jade hall' and 'strictly guarded.'
  • 见五真喜曰来何晚耶 - Describes a greeting, with 'seeing the Five Immortals and saying with joy' and 'why did you come so late.'
  • 饮以玉杯赐以金简凤文之衣玉华之冠 - Describes a grand reception, with 'drinking from a jade cup,' 'giving a golden tablet,' 'clothes with phoenix patterns,' and 'a jade crown.'
  • 配居蓬莱华院四人者出 - Describes a distribution of residence, with 'being assigned to live in the beautiful halls of Mount Penglai' and 'the four people leave.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 修真 - Likely a Daoist or hermit figure, possibly fictional or symbolic.
  • 守真 - Likely a Daoist or hermit figure, possibly fictional or symbolic.
  • 敬真 - Likely a Daoist or hermit figure, possibly fictional or symbolic.
  • 华岳 - Mount Hua, a sacred mountain in Chinese mythology.
  • 东瀛 - The East Sea, often associated with the sea of the immortals.
  • 蓬莱 - Mount Penglai, a mythical paradise in Chinese mythology.
  • 大仙伯 - The Great Immortal Lord, likely a Daoist deity or figure.
  • 茅君 - Lord Mao, likely referring to a Daoist figure, possibly associated with the Five Immortals.
  • 五真 - The Five Immortals, likely a group of Daoist deities or figures.
  • 金阙玉堂 - Golden gate and jade hall, symbolic of a divine or heavenly abode.
  • 王父 - The old father, likely a metaphor for a high-ranking figure or a deity.

[812]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 忘王父也惟仙伯哀之 - Forgetting the father, only the immortal elder mourned him. The phrase uses '忘' (forget) and '哀之' (mourn for him) to express a sense of loss and sorrow.
  • 汝当其防无自坠其道 - You should follow his path without letting yourself fall from it. Here, '当其防' (follow his path) and '无自坠其道' (do not let yourself fall from the way) convey the idea of staying true to a legacy or tradition.
  • 因敕四真送至其家 - Therefore, he ordered four immortals to send him to his home. '敕' (order) and '四真' (four immortals) are used to describe a supernatural intervention.
  • 此性也非学也 - This is nature, not learning. The phrase emphasizes innate qualities over acquired knowledge.
  • 云来迎即去不来亦无术可召 - If they come to fetch me, I will go; if they do not come, I have no method to summon them. This reflects a passive acceptance of fate.
  • 谢絶其夫服黄冠 - He thanked and rejected his husband, wearing a yellow crown. '谢絶' (thank and reject) and '黄冠' (yellow crown) indicate a formal and symbolic gesture.
  • 邯以状闻州州闻亷使时崔従按察陕辅延之舍扵陕州紫极宫请王父扵别室 - Han reported the situation to the state, which informed the censor. At that time, Cui Cong was inspecting Shanfu. Han invited the immortal father to a separate room in the Purple Extreme Palace in Yanzhou. This describes a chain of official actions and a specific location.
  • 人不得升其阶惟亷使従事及夫人得之瞻拜者才及阶而已亦不得升 - No one could ascend the stairs except the censor and his wife, who could only reach the bottom step and not go further. This highlights the restricted access to the immortal father.
  • 唐宪宗召见舍扵内殿试道而无以对罢之 - Emperor Xianzong of Tang summoned him to the inner palace, tested his Daoist knowledge, but he had nothing to say and was dismissed. '试道' (test Daoist knowledge) and '无以对' (had nothing to say) describe a failed attempt at demonstrating expertise.
  • 今在陕州终嵗不食食时防果实或饮酒二三杯绝无所食但容色转芳嫩耳 - Now in Yanzhou, he does not eat all year. When he eats, he only consumes a few fruits or drinks two or three cups of wine, but he does not eat anything else. His appearance becomes more fragrant and tender. This describes a lifestyle of minimal sustenance and physical transformation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王父 - Refers to a revered ancestor or father, often used in a respectful context.
  • 仙伯 - An immortal elder, a figure in Daoist mythology.
  • 四真 - Four immortals, significant figures in Daoist tradition.
  • 黄冠 - A yellow crown, a symbol of Daoist priests or immortals.
  • 崔従 - Cui Cong, a historical figure who served as a censor during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 陕辅 - Shanfu, a region in Shaanxi Province.
  • 紫极宫 - Purple Extreme Palace, a Daoist temple in Yanzhou.
  • 唐宪宗 - Emperor Xianzong of Tang, the 12th emperor of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 内殿 - Inner palace, a place of high status within the imperial court.
  • 道 - Dao, the fundamental principle or way in Daoist philosophy.

Proper Nouns

  • 王父 - Revered ancestor or father.
  • 仙伯 - Immortal elder.
  • 四真 - Four immortals.
  • 黄冠 - Yellow crown.
  • 崔従 - Cui Cong.
  • 陕辅 - Shanfu.
  • 紫极宫 - Purple Extreme Palace.
  • 唐宪宗 - Emperor Xianzong of Tang.
  • 内殿 - Inner palace.
  • 道 - Dao.

[813]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 寳厯中有封陟孝亷者 - During the Bao Li period, there was a person named Feng Zhi, who served as a Xiaolian (孝亷). The term '孝亷' (Xiaolian) refers to a type of official selection system in ancient China, where candidates were chosen based on their filial piety and integrity.
  • 貎态洁朗 - Describes Feng Zhi's physical appearance, emphasizing his cleanliness and brightness.
  • 性颇贞端 - Describes Feng Zhi's moral character, emphasizing his sincerity and uprightness.
  • 志在典坟 - Describes Feng Zhi's aspirations, emphasizing his focus on the classics.
  • 僻于林薮 - Describes Feng Zhi's living environment, emphasizing his seclusion in nature.
  • 探义而星归腐草 - Metaphorical expression suggesting that Feng Zhi's pursuit of knowledge was as constant and reliable as the stars returning to their resting place.
  • 阅经而月坠幽 - Metaphorical expression suggesting that Feng Zhi's study was intense and profound, like the moon descending into a dark room.
  • 兀兀孜孜俾夜作昼 - Describes Feng Zhi's dedication to his studies, emphasizing his hard work and persistence.
  • 无非捜索隐奥 - Describes Feng Zhi's continuous pursuit of knowledge and understanding.
  • 未尝暂纵愒时日也 - Emphasizes Feng Zhi's diligence and his efficient use of time.
  • 书堂之畔景像可窥 - Describes the view from Feng Zhi's study, emphasizing the natural beauty of his surroundings.
  • 泉石清寒桂兰雅淡 - Describes the natural beauty and tranquility of Feng Zhi's environment.
  • 戱猱每窃其庭果 - Describes the playful and mischievous nature of the monkeys in Feng Zhi's environment.
  • 唳频栖扵涧松 - Describes the peaceful and serene atmosphere of Feng Zhi's surroundings.
  • 虚时吟 - Describes Feng Zhi's habit of reciting poetry, emphasizing his love for literature.
  • 纎埃昼閴 - Describes the peaceful and tranquil atmosphere of Feng Zhi's environment.
  • 烟锁筜篁之翠节 - Describes the natural beauty and tranquility of Feng Zhi's surroundings.
  • 露滋踯躅之红葩 - Describes the natural beauty and tranquility of Feng Zhi's surroundings.
  • 薜蔓衣垣 - Describes the natural beauty and tranquility of Feng Zhi's surroundings.
  • 苔茸毯砌 - Describes the natural beauty and tranquility of Feng Zhi's surroundings.
  • 忽飘异香 - Describes an unexpected and unusual event.
  • 酷烈渐布扵庭际 - Describes the spread of the fragrance.
  • 俄有辎軿自空而降 - Describes an extraordinary and supernatural event.
  • 画轮轧轧 - Describes the sound of the carriage.
  • 直凑檐楹 - Describes the carriage's approach.
  • 见一仙姝 - Describes the appearance of a fairy.
  • 侍従华丽 - Describes the attendants of the fairy.
  • 玉佩敲磬 - Describes the sound of the jade pendants.
  • 罗裙曵云 - Describes the fairy's appearance, emphasizing her ethereal beauty.
  • 体欺皓雪之容光 - Describes the fairy's appearance, emphasizing her purity and radiance.
  • 脸夺芙蕖之艶冶 - Describes the fairy's appearance, emphasizing her beauty.
  • 正容敛袵而揖陟 - Describes the fairy's greeting, emphasizing her grace and politeness.

  • 须结队执兵而入 - '须' means 'must,' '结队' means 'form a group,' '执兵' means 'hold weapons,' and '而入' means 'enter.' Indicates that people must form a group and hold weapons to enter.
  • 姑隐其间曽无怖畏 - '姑隐其间' means 'the person hid among them,' '曽' means 'ever,' and '无怖畏' means 'no fear.' Indicates that the person never felt afraid.
  • 数年后有一青鸟形如鸠鸽 - '数年后' means 'after several years,' '有一青鸟' means 'there was a blue bird,' and '形如鸠鸽' means 'shaped like a pigeon.' Indicates that after several years, there was a blue bird shaped like a pigeon.
  • 红顶长尾飞来所居 - '红顶' means 'red head,' '长尾' means 'long tail,' and '飞来所居' means 'flew to where the person lived.' Indicates that the bird flew to where the person lived.
  • 自语云我南岳夫人使也 - '自语' means 'spoke to itself,' '云' means 'said,' '我南岳夫人使也' means 'I am the messenger of Lady Nanyue.' Indicates that the bird said it was the messenger of Lady Nanyue.
  • 以姑修道精苦独栖穷林命我为伴 - '以姑修道精苦' means 'because the person practiced Taoism with great effort,' '独栖穷林' means 'lived alone in a remote forest,' and '命我为伴' means 'sent me as a companion.' Indicates that the bird was sent as a companion because the person practiced Taoism with great effort and lived alone in a remote forest.
  • 他日又言西王母姓缑乃姑之祖也 - '他日' means 'another day,' '又言' means 'also said,' '西王母' is a proper name, '姓缑' means 'last name is Gong,' and '乃姑之祖也' means 'is the ancestor of the person.' Indicates that another day, the bird said that the person's ancestor was Lady Xi Wangmu, who had the last name Gong.
  • 闻姑修道勤至将有真官降而授道 - '闻' means 'heard,' '姑修道勤至' means 'the person practiced Taoism diligently,' '将有真官降' means 'there will be a true official descending,' and '授道' means 'teach Taoism.' Indicates that the bird heard that the person practiced Taoism diligently and that a true official would descend to teach Taoism.
  • 但时未至耳宜勉扵修励也 - '但' means 'but,' '时未至' means 'the time has not come,' '耳' means 'indeed,' '宜勉扵修励' means 'should strive to practice diligently.' Indicates that the time has not come, but the person should strive to practice diligently.
  • 每有人逰山必青鸟先言其姓字 - '每' means 'every time,' '有人逰山' means 'someone visits the mountain,' '必' means 'always,' '青鸟先言其姓字' means 'the blue bird always says their name.' Indicates that every time someone visits the mountain, the blue bird always says their name.
  • 又曰河南缑氏乃王母修道之故山也 - '又曰' means 'also said,' '河南' is a place name, '缑氏' is a proper name, '乃' means 'is,' '王母' is a proper name, '修道之故山' means 'the old mountain where she practiced Taoism.' Indicates that the bird also said that the old mountain where Lady Xi Wangmu practiced Taoism is in Henan, and the person's ancestor is from there.
  • 又一日青鸟飞来曰今夕 - '又一日' means 'another day,' '青鸟飞来' means 'the blue bird flew in,' and '曰今夕' means 'said today evening.' Indicates that another day, the blue bird flew in and said today evening.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 同曰无惧也即移舟及之 - Direct command: 'do not fear, just move the boat to meet them.'
  • 官吏皆拜同曰未也犹在春中但去无速也 - Complex phrase: 'the officials all bowed, indicating respect or submission; not yet, still in spring, just leave without haste.'
  • 遂各去同舟者莫测之 - Elliptical phrase: 'they then parted ways, and those who were on the same boat could not understand it, suggesting a mysterious or supernatural event.'
  • 明年二月同沐浴饵紫虚所授之丹 - Sequence of actions: 'the following February, 同 took a bath and ate the elixir given by Zixu, indicating a spiritual or alchemical practice.'
  • 二仙女亦宻降其室十四日称疾而卒 - Supernatural event: 'two fairy maidens also secretly descended into his room, and on the fourteenth day, he claimed to be ill and died.'
  • 有仙三十六只翔集庭宇形质柔缓状若生人额中有白光一防良久化为紫气 - Mystical transformation: 'there were thirty-six fairy cranes flying and gathering in the courtyard, their forms were soft and gentle, resembling living beings, they had a white light on their foreheads that lasted for a long time and then turned into purple vapor.'
  • 沐浴之际髪重生立长数寸 - Miraculous recovery: 'during the bath, his black hair grew back and stood up several inches.'
  • 十五日夜云彩满空忽尔雷电棺葢飞在庭中失尸所在空衣而已 - Supernatural event: 'on the fifteenth night, the sky was filled with clouds, suddenly there was thunder and lightning, the coffin lid flew into the courtyard, the body was nowhere to be found, only the empty clothes remained.'
  • 异香羣浃旬不休 - Prolonged mystical phenomenon: 'a strange fragrance and a flock of cranes lasted for ten days without stopping.'
  • 时僖宗在蜀浙西节度使周寳表其事诏付史官 - Historical record: 'at that time, Emperor Xizong was in Sichuan, Zhou Bao, the governor of western Zhejiang, reported the matter and ordered it to be recorded by the historians.'
  • 戚逍遥兾州南宫人也父以教授自资逍遥十余嵗好道清淡不为儿戯父母亦好道常行隂徳父以女诫授逍遥逍遥曰此常人之事耳遂取老子仙经诵之 - Shift in spiritual focus: 'Qi Xiaoyao was from Nan Gong in Xizhou, his father supported himself by teaching, in his teens, he loved the Dao and was indifferent to worldly pleasures, his parents also loved the Dao and often performed good deeds, his father taught him the *Book of Filial Piety*, but Qi Xiaoyao said, 'These are just ordinary things,' and then took the *Laozi* and chanted it.'
  • 年二十余适同邑蒯浔舅姑酷责之以农怠惰而逍遥旦夕以斋洁修行为事殊不以生计在心蒯浔亦屡责之逍遥白舅姑请返扵父母及父母家亦逼廹终以不能为尘俗事愿独居小室修道以资舅姑蒯浔及舅姑俱疑乃弃之扵室而逍 - Conflict between spiritual and worldly responsibilities: 'in his twenties, he married a woman from the same town named Kuai Xun, his mother-in-law and father-in-law severely criticized him for being lazy in farming, but Qi Xiaoyao devoted himself to fasting, cleanliness, and spiritual practice, not caring about his livelihood, Kuai Xun also criticized him, and Qi Xiaoyao told his in-laws that he wanted to return to his parents' house. His parents also pressured him, and he finally decided not to engage in worldly affairs, wishing to live alone in a small room to practice the Dao to support his in-laws. Kuai Xun and his in-laws doubted him and abandoned him in the room.'

[838]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 尤好经史 - 尤好 means especially fond of, and 经史 refers to the classics and historical texts. Together, it indicates a deep interest in classical literature and history.
  • 披阅清净为事 - 披阅 means to read through or peruse, and 清净为事 means to take reading as a pure and peaceful activity. This phrase describes the act of reading as a serene and focused pursuit.
  • 赍府帅命 - 赍 means to carry or bear, and 府帅命 means the command of the governor. This phrase indicates that Zhang Jianzhang was carrying a message or order from the governor.
  • 忽有青衣泛一叶舟而至 - 忽有 means suddenly there was, 青衣 refers to a servant or attendant dressed in blue, 泛 means to float or drift, and 一叶舟 means a small boat. This phrase describes the sudden appearance of a servant in a small boat.
  • 奉大仙命 - 奉 means to follow or obey, and 大仙命 means the command of a great immortal. This phrase indicates that the servant is following the command of a deity.
  • 请大夫 - 请 means to invite or request, and 大夫 is a title for a high-ranking official or scholar. This phrase means to invite the gentleman or request the gentleman.
  • 勿患风涛之苦 - 勿患 means do not worry about, 风涛 means wind and waves, and 之苦 means the hardship. This phrase reassures Zhang Jianzhang not to worry about the dangers of the sea.
  • 吾令此青衣往来导之 - 吾 means I, 令 means to order, 此青衣 means this servant, 往来 means to come and go, and 导之 means to guide him. This phrase means I will order this servant to guide you.
  • 及廻至西岸 - 及 means when, 廻 means return, and 至西岸 means to the western shore. This phrase indicates the return to the western shore.
  • 经太宗征辽碑 - 经 means to pass by, 太宗 refers to Emperor Taizong of Tang, 征辽 means campaign against Liao, and 碑 means stele or stone monument. This phrase describes passing by a stele commemorating Emperor Taizong's campaign against Liao.
  • 半没水中 - 半没 means half submerged, and 水中 means in the water. This phrase describes the stele being partially submerged in the water.
  • 以帛裹面摸而读之 - 以帛裹面 means with a piece of silk wrapped around the face, and 摸而读之 means to touch and read it. This phrase describes the act of touching and reading the stele.
  • 不失一字 - 不失 means not missing, and 一字 means a single character. This phrase indicates that Zhang Jianzhang did not miss a single character.
  • 其笃学如此 - 其 means his, 笃学 means diligent in study, and 如此 means like this. This phrase summarizes Zhang Jianzhang's dedication to learning.
  • 蓟门之人皆能说之 - 蓟门 refers to the area around Jixian (modern-day Tianjin), 之人 means the people, and 皆能说之 means all can tell about it. This phrase indicates that the people of Jixian all know about Zhang Jianzhang's story.
  • 周寳为浙西节度使 - 周寳 is the name of the person, 为 means to be, 浙西 refers to the western part of Zhejiang, and 节度使 means military governor. This phrase introduces Zhou Bao as the military governor of western Zhejiang.
  • 治城隍 - 治 means to govern, and 城隍 means city walls and moats. This phrase describes Zhou Bao's efforts to improve the city's defenses.
  • 棺椟将腐 - 棺椟 means coffin, and 将腐 means about to rot. This phrase indicates that the coffin was deteriorating.
  • 掌役者以告寳 - 掌役者 means the person in charge of the work, 以告 means to report to, and 寳 is the name of the person. This phrase describes the person in charge reporting the discovery to Zhou Bao.
  • 或曰 - 或曰 means someone said or it was said. This phrase introduces a comment or opinion.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 贡明经举 - A candidate who passed the *mingjing* examination; formal title.
  • 贫无仆従 - Poor and without attendants; *従* is a variant of *从*, meaning 'to follow' or 'attendant'.
  • 自驾其驴 - To drive his own donkey; in context, means he rides his own donkey.
  • 断缰而走 - Broke the halter and ran off; *缰* is a halter or reins, *走* is to run or flee.
  • 甚熟此驴直上 - Very familiar with this donkey, it went straight up; elliptical phrase.
  • 颇修敬焉 - He showed great respect; *修敬* is a formal expression of respect or reverence.
  • 声技皆仙者 - Their music and performance were those of immortals; *声技* refers to music and performance, *仙者* is 'immortals' or 'celestial beings'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 唐开元中 - During the Kaiyuan period of the Tang dynasty; *Kaiyuan* era (713–741 CE) was a golden age of the Tang.
  • 明经举 - The *mingjing* examination was one of the main categories of the imperial examination system, focusing on Confucian classics.
  • 终南山 - Zongmashan Mountain, a famous mountain in Shaanxi province, often associated with Daoist and Buddhist hermits and immortals.
  • 仙者 - Immortals or celestial beings; common motif in Daoist and Buddhist texts, representing a transcendent or otherworldly realm.

Proper Nouns

  • 陆生 - Mr. Lu or Lu the scholar; classical way of referring to a scholar or gentleman.
  • 终南山 - Zongmashan Mountain (or Mount Zhongnan); proper noun, should be left as is.
  • 启夏门 - Qixia Gate; likely a gate in Chang’an, should be left as is unless a specific location is confirmed.
  • 明经 - Mingjing; a title of an imperial examination, should be left as is and explained in context.

  • 但心存吾约无虑也 - Means 'just keep my agreement in mind and there will be no worries.'

Idiomatic Expressions

This suggests a supernatural or mysterious event.
  • 悮入户部王侍郎宅 - '悮入' (mistakenly enter) indicates that the protagonist entered the wrong place by mistake.
  • 复入阁正见一女临镜晨妆 - '复入阁' (again entered the hall) and '正见一女临镜晨妆' (just saw a woman dressing by the mirror in the morning) describe the protagonist's next action and what he saw.
  • 投杖于牀擕女而去 - '投杖于牀' (threw his staff on the bed) and '擕女而去' (took the woman away) describe the protagonist's actions after seeing the woman.
  • 比下阶顾见竹已化作女形僵卧在床 - '比下阶' (as he descended the steps) and '顾见竹已化作女形僵卧在床' (looked back and saw the bamboo had transformed into a woman's form lying stiffly on the bed) describe the protagonist's reaction and what he saw.
  • 一家惊呼云小娘子卒亡生将女去 - '一家惊呼' (the whole family cried out) and '云小娘子卒亡生将女去' (said that the young lady had died and the man had taken her away) describe the family's reaction.
  • 防侍郎下朝旹权要请谒盈街宅门重不得出隐扵中门侧 - '防侍郎下朝' (to prevent the Vice Minister from coming home), '权要请谒盈街' (officials and important people were full of requests), '宅门重不得出' (the mansion gates were heavily guarded and could not be opened), and '隐扵中门侧' (hid on the side of the middle gate) describe the situation and the protagonist's actions.
  • 王闻女亡入宅省视左右奔走不絶须臾公卿以下皆至门矣 - '王闻女亡' (the Vice Minister heard that the girl had died), '入宅省视' (entered the house to inspect), '左右奔走不絶' (people were running around non-stop), and '须臾公卿以下皆至门矣' (soon, officials and below all arrived at the gate) describe the Vice Minister's actions and the reaction of others.
  • 叶天师在朝奔遣屈生隐扵户下半日矣 - '叶天师在朝' (the Taoist priest Ye was in court), '奔遣屈生' (sent someone to summon the man), '隐扵户下半日矣' (hid himself under the half-day) describe the actions of the Taoist priest and the protagonist.
  • 少顷叶天师至诊视之曰此非魅乃道术者为之耳遂取水喷呪死女立变为竹 - '少顷叶天师至' (a short while later, the Taoist priest arrived), '诊视之' (examined it), '曰此非魅乃道术者为之耳' (said that this was not a ghost but a trick by a Daoist practitioner), '遂取水喷呪' (then took water and sprinkled it while reciting a spell), '死女立变为竹' (the dead woman immediately turned back into bamboo) describe the Taoist priest's actions and the result.
  • 又曰此亦不逺搜尚在遂持刀禁呪绕宅寻索果扵门侧 - '又曰此亦不逺搜尚在' (then said that it was not far away and still there), '遂持刀禁呪' (then took a knife and recited a spell), '绕宅寻索' (searched around the house), '果扵门侧' (indeed found it on the side of the gate) describe the Taoist priest's further actions and the result.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 火销之探懐中取葫芦泻出两丸药以物搅之少顷去火已成上银曰此合得万余贯修观计用有余搅则所获无多但罢之 - Highly compact and elliptical phrase describing a process of alchemy or transformation: '火销之' likely refers to a fire or heat process, '探懐中取葫芦泻出两丸药' describes retrieving and pouring out two pills from a gourd, '以物搅之' means stirring with something, '少顷去火已成上银' suggests that after a short time, the fire is removed and silver is produced. '此合得万余贯修观计用有余' means that this process can yield more than ten thousand strings of coins, which is more than enough for temple repairs. '搅则所获无多但罢之' suggests that if one stirs too much, the yield will be small, so it's best to stop.
  • 黄生与徒弟皆相谢问其所欲笑出门去不知所之后十余年黄生奉诏赴京忽扵长街西见揷骡鞭者肩一幞子随骑驴老人行全无茅山气色 - Narrative passage: '黄生与徒弟皆相谢问其所欲' means that Huang Sheng and his disciples all thanked him and asked what he wanted. '笑出门去不知所之' means he laughed and left without saying where he was going. '后十余年黄生奉诏赴京忽扵长街西见揷骡鞭者肩一幞子随骑驴老人行全无茅山气色' describes Huang Sheng, after many years, being summoned to the capital and suddenly seeing a man with a donkey whip and a bundle on his shoulder, followed by an elderly man riding a donkey, who showed no signs of being from Mount Mao.
  • 仙人许君居世之时尝因修观功用既毕欲刻石记之因得古碑文字刓缺不可复识因刬去旧文刋勒新记自是恍惚不安暇日徐歩庭砌闻空中言曰许君许君速诣水官求救不然即有不测之衅许愕然异之又问其事杳不复荅乃焚香防祀愿示求救之由良久复语曰所刻碑旧文虽已磨没而此时为文之人见诣水官相讼云夺我之名己之名由此水官将有执对之命速宜求之许君乃讶得旧文立石刋纪一夕梦神人相谢再顕名氏无以相报请作水陆大醮普告山水万灵得三官举名可以证道许君依教修之遂成道果自此水陆醮法传扵人间 - Detailed narrative about Xu Jun, a celestial being: '仙人许君居世之时尝因修观功用既毕欲刻石记之' means that when Xu Jun lived in the world, after completing the construction of a temple, he wanted to carve an inscription on a stone to record it. '因得古碑文字刓缺不可复识因刬去旧文刋勒新记' means that he found an ancient stele with worn and unreadable text, so he erased the old text and carved a new inscription. '自是恍惚不安暇日徐歩庭砌闻空中言曰许君许君速诣水官求救不然即有不测之衅' means that after this, he became restless and uneasy. One day, while slowly walking in the courtyard, he heard a voice from the air saying, 'Xu Jun, Xu Jun, hurry to the Water Official for help, otherwise you will face unforeseen misfortune.' '许愕然异之又问其事杳不复荅乃焚香防祀愿示求救之由良久复语曰所刻碑旧文虽已磨没而此时为文之人见诣水官相讼云夺我之名己之名由此水官将有执对之命速宜求之' means that Xu Jun was startled and asked what was happening, but the voice did not reply. He then burned incense and offered sacrifices, hoping to show the reason for seeking help. After a long time, the voice spoke again, saying that the old text on the stele, though worn away, had been claimed by someone who was now accusing him of stealing their name and reputation, and that the Water Official would have a judgment against him, so he should seek help quickly. '许君乃讶得旧文立石刋纪一夕梦神人相谢再顕名氏无以相报请作水陆大醮普告山水万灵得三官举名可以证道许君依教修之遂成道果自此水陆醮法传扵人间' means that Xu Jun was surprised and found the old text, which he then carved on a stone. That night, he dreamed of a divine being thanking him and revealing their name, saying there was nothing to repay him with but asking him to perform a water and land grand offering to inform all the spirits of mountains and waters, and that with the names of the Three Officials, he could prove his way. Xu Jun followed the instruction and practiced it, achieving enlightenment, and from then on, the method of water and land offerings spread among people.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 非已畜何容便货 - Elliptical phrase meaning 'How can something that is not yet owned be sold immediately?' It questions the ethics or practicality of selling something before it is fully owned.
  • 况絷之山门今已一月 - Means 'Moreover, it has been tied up at the mountain gate for a month.' It indicates a situation where something has been confined or restrained for a considerable time.
  • 其存亡不可知也 - Means 'Its existence or non-existence is unknown.' It expresses uncertainty about the status of something.
  • 第依吾教勿过忧苦 - Means 'Just follow our teachings and do not worry excessively.' It suggests a directive to adhere to certain instructions while maintaining composure.
  • 操即出山宛见其驴尚在 - Means 'Cao immediately went out of the mountain and saw that his donkey was still there.' It describes a specific action and observation.
  • 还乗之而驰足力甚壮 - Means 'He rode it again and found its strength very robust.' It describes the physical condition of the donkey.
  • 货之果得五千 - Means 'He sold it and indeed received five thousand.' It indicates a successful transaction.
  • 因探懐中二叟所示之书惟买水银耳 - Means 'He then took out the book shown to him by the two old men, which only mentioned buying mercury.' It describes the content of the book.
  • 操即为交易薄晚而归 - Means 'Cao immediately made the transaction and returned late in the evening.' It describes the timing and outcome of the transaction.
  • 终暝遂及二叟之舍 - Means 'Finally, it was dark, and he reached the two old men's dwelling.' It describes the end of the day and the arrival at a specific location.
  • 二叟即以杂药烧炼俄而化为黄金 - Means 'The two old men immediately used various medicines to smelt and refine it, and soon it turned into gold.' It describes a magical transformation.
  • 因以此术示之扵操 - Means 'They then showed this technique to Cao.' It describes the demonstration of a skill.
  • 自尔半年二叟徐谓操曰 - Means 'For half a year, the two old men gradually told Cao.' It describes a prolonged period of instruction.
  • 汝可归寜三年之后当与汝防扵茅庐 - Means 'You can return home for three years, and after that, we will meet you at the thatched cottage.' It describes a future plan.
  • 操愿防不获于是辞诀 - Means 'Cao wished to meet but did not get the chance, so he bid farewell.' It describes a missed opportunity and a parting.
  • 及家相国薨再宿矣 - Means 'Upon reaching home, the prime minister had already passed away, and he stayed for two more nights.' It describes a personal and political event.
  • 操过小祥则又入山 - Means 'Cao passed the small mourning period and then entered the mountain again.' It describes a ritual observance and a subsequent action.
  • 岐路木石峯峦树木皆非向之所经也 - Means 'The paths, mountains, rocks, peaks, and trees were all different from what he had previously encountered.' It describes a change in the environment.
  • 操亟返服阕因告别昆弟逰扵江湖 - Means 'Cao hurriedly returned to mourn and then bid farewell to his brothers to travel on the rivers and lakes.' It describes a series of actions and a decision.
  • 至今无覊扵世従学道者甚众操终无传焉 - Means 'Up to now, there are many who follow the path of learning, but Cao never passed it on.' It describes the legacy of Cao's teachings.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • Long sentences can be broken down into several parts for clarity. For example, '非已畜何容便货况絷之山门今已一月其存亡不可知也' can be translated as 'How can something that is not yet owned be sold immediately? Moreover, it has been tied up at the mountain gate for a month, and its existence or non-existence is unknown.'
  • Another example is '操即出山宛见其驴尚在还乗之而驰足力甚壮货之果得五千因探懐中二叟所示之书惟买水银耳,' which can be translated as 'Cao immediately went out of the mountain and saw that his donkey was still there. He rode it again and found its strength very robust. He sold it and indeed received five thousand. He then took out the book shown to him by the two old men, which only mentioned buying mercury.'
  • Similarly, '操即为交易薄晚而归终暝遂及二叟之舍二叟即以杂药烧炼俄而化为黄金因以此术示之扵操' can be translated as 'Cao immediately made the transaction and returned late in the evening. Finally, it was dark, and he reached the two old men's dwelling. The two old men immediately used various medicines to smelt and refine it, and soon it turned into gold. They then showed this technique to Cao.'
  • For '自尔半年二叟徐谓操曰汝可归寜三年之后当与汝防扵茅庐,' the translation is 'For half a year, the two old men gradually told Cao, 'You can return home for three years, and after that, we will meet you at the thatched cottage.'
  • And '操愿防不获于是辞诀及家相国薨再宿矣' can be translated as 'Cao wished to meet but did not get the chance, so he bid farewell. Upon reaching home, the prime minister had already passed away, and he stayed for two more nights.'
  • For '操过小祥则又入山岐路木石峯峦树木皆非向之所经也,' the translation is 'Cao passed the small mourning period and then entered the mountain again. The paths, mountains, rocks, peaks, and trees were all different from what he had previously encountered.'
  • Finally, '操亟返服阕因告别昆弟逰扵江湖至今无覊扵世従学道者甚众操终无传焉' can be translated as 'Cao hurriedly returned to mourn and then bid farewell to his brothers to travel on the rivers and lakes. Up to now, there are many who follow the path of learning, but Cao never passed it on.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 我当有药如此者数四 - Indicates that the speaker has had this kind of medicine several times before; could be interpreted as 'I have had such medicine several times' or 'I have had this kind of medicine four times.'
  • 夜后遂扃门 - '夜后' means 'after nightfall,' and '扃门' means 'to lock the door'; suggests that the action of locking the door occurs after nightfall.
  • 擕一小锅入陈生宻窥之 - '擕' means 'to carry,' '一小锅' means 'a small pot,' and '宻窥之' means 'to secretly observe it'; describes the act of carrying a small pot and secretly observing something.
  • 见扵葫芦中泻水银数合 - '见扵葫芦中' means 'seeing in the gourd,' and '泻水银数合' means 'pouring several dou of mercury'; describes the act of pouring mercury from a gourd.
  • 煎之搅如稀饧 - '煎之' means 'to boil it,' and '搅如稀饧' means 'stirring it like thin molasses'; describes the process of boiling and stirring the mercury.
  • 投一丸药乃为金矣 - '投一丸药' means 'to throw in a pill of medicine,' and '乃为金矣' means 'and it becomes gold'; describes the transformation of the medicine into gold.
  • 佣者撚両丸以裹置懐中 - '佣者' means 'the laborer,' '撚両丸' means 'to pinch two pills,' '以裹' means 'wrapped in cloth,' and '置懐中' means 'placed in his bosom'; describes the laborer pinching two pills, wrapping them in cloth, and placing them in his bosom.
  • 明日日髙起求药者已至 - '明日日髙起' means 'the next day, at dawn,' and '求药者已至' means 'the person seeking medicine has arrived'; describes the arrival of the person seeking medicine the next morning.
  • 乃持丸者付之令患齿者含之一丸未半乃平复矣 - '乃持丸者付之' means 'then the person holding the pills gave them,' '令患齿者含之' means 'ordered the person with tooth pain to hold one in their mouth,' and '一丸未半乃平复矣' means 'before half a pill was consumed, the pain subsided'; describes the process of giving the pill and the rapid relief of the tooth pain.
  • 痛止第出虫数十 - '痛止' means 'the pain stopped,' and '第出虫数十' means 'only dozens of worms came out'; describes the cessation of pain and the emergence of worms.
  • 陈生伺佣者出于房内搜而观之得书二卷不喻其防遂藏之 - '陈生伺佣者出于房内' means 'Chen Sheng waited until the laborer left the room,' '搜而观之' means 'searched and observed it,' '得书二卷' means 'found two scrolls of books,' and '不喻其防遂藏之' means 'did not understand their meaning and thus hid them'; describes Chen Sheng's actions after the laborer left.
  • 佣者至大怒骂陈生生不敢隐却还之曰某今去矣遂出门入水沐浴乃变为美少年无复疮疥也 - '佣者至大怒骂陈生' means 'the laborer arrived and angrily cursed Chen Sheng,' '生不敢隐却还之' means 'Chen Sheng dared not hide it and returned it,' '曰某今去矣' means 'saying 'I will leave now,' '遂出门入水沐浴' means 'then went out, entered the water, and bathed,' and '乃变为美少年无复疮疥也' means 'and became a handsome young man without any more sores or scabs'; describes the laborer's reaction and transformation.
  • 拜讫跳入深涧中遂不知所之 - '拜讫' means 'after bowing,' '跳入深涧中' means 'jumped into a deep ravine,' and '遂不知所之' means 'and was never seen again'; describes the final action of the transformed individual.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '我当有药如此者数四' is somewhat ambiguous and could be interpreted as 'I have had such medicine several times' or 'I have had this kind of medicine four times.'
  • The sequence of actions in '夜后遂扃门炽火擕一小锅入陈生宻窥之' is clear but could be read differently depending on the emphasis. It could be interpreted as 'After nightfall, he locked the door, lit a fire, carried a small pot, and secretly observed Chen Sheng.'
  • The sequence of actions in '见扵葫芦中泻水银数合煎之搅如稀饧' is clear but could be read differently depending on the emphasis. It could be interpreted as 'He saw in the gourd several dou of mercury being poured, boiled, and stirred like thin molasses.'
  • The transformation in '投一丸药乃为金矣' is clear but could be read differently depending on the emphasis. It could be interpreted as 'He threw in a pill of medicine, and it became gold.'
  • The sequence of actions in '佣者撚両丸以裹置懐中' is clear but could be read differently depending on the emphasis. It could be interpreted as 'The laborer pinched two pills, wrapped them in cloth, and placed them in his bosom.'
  • The sequence of actions in '余作一金饼密赍出门去' is clear but could be read differently depending on the emphasis. It could be interpreted as 'I made a golden cake, secretly carried it, and went out the door.'
  • The sequence of events in '明日日髙起求药者已至' is clear but could be read differently depending on the emphasis. It could be interpreted as 'The next day, at dawn, the person seeking medicine had arrived.'
  • The sequence of actions in '乃持丸者付之令患齿者含之一丸未半乃平复矣' is clear but could be read differently depending on the emphasis. It could be interpreted as 'Then the person holding the pills gave them, ordered the person with tooth pain to hold one in their mouth, and before half a pill was consumed, the pain subsided.'
  • The sequence of events in '痛止第出虫数十' is clear but could be read differently depending on the emphasis. It could be interpreted as 'The pain stopped, and only dozens of worms came out.'
  • The sequence of actions in '陈生伺佣者出于房内搜而观之得书二卷不喻其防遂藏之' is clear but could be read differently depending on the emphasis. It could be interpreted as 'Chen Sheng waited until the laborer left the room, searched and observed it, found two scrolls of books, did not understand their meaning, and thus hid them.'
  • The sequence of actions in '佣者至大怒骂陈生生不敢隐却还之曰某今去矣遂出门入水沐浴乃变为美少年无复疮疥也' is clear but could be read differently depending on the emphasis. It could be interpreted as 'The laborer arrived and angrily cursed Chen Sheng, who dared not hide it and returned it, saying 'I will leave now,' then went out, entered the water, and bathed, and became a handsome young man without any more sores or scabs.'
  • The sequence of actions in '拜讫跳入深涧中遂不知所之' is clear but could be read differently depending on the emphasis. It could be interpreted as 'After bowing, he jumped into a deep ravine and was never seen again.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '有纎隙又命以筯数百呼其僮绳而架之且告客曰' is a complex sequence of actions and instructions, requiring careful segmentation.
  • The sequence '数客歩庭中且伺焉忽觉天地曛晦仰而视之即又无纎云俄闻生呼曰某至矣' describes a series of events and observations, requiring clear temporal and causal relationships.
  • The sequence '月在某衣中耳请客观焉因以举之其衣中出月寸许忽一室尽明寒逼肌' describes a surprising and dramatic event, requiring careful attention to the order of actions and their effects.
  • The sequence '子不信我今信乎客再拜谢之愿收其光因又闭户其外尚昏晦食顷方如初' describes the guests' reactions and the return to normalcy, requiring clear transitions and logical flow.
  • The sentence '韩志和本倭国人也中国为飞龙卫士善雕木为鸾鸟雀之形置机捩于腹中发之则飞高三二百尺数百步外方始却下' describes Han Zhihe's skills and the functioning of his creations, requiring clear and concise explanations.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 曽至郭恩家 - Classical Chinese expression meaning 'Once visited the house of Guo En'; '曽' is a Classical Chinese particle meaning 'once' or 'ever'.
  • 鸣甚悲切 - 'Sang with great sorrow'; '鸣' is used metaphorically to convey emotional tone.
  • 相探擕豕及酒 - 'To visit, carrying a pig and wine'; '探' may be a variant or typo for '探訪' (to visit); '豕' is an old term for domestic pig.
  • 流血惊怖 - 'Bled and was terrified'; '惊怖' is a compound term for 'terrified' or 'shocked'.
  • 合诸药总纳一竹筒内 - 'Combined various medicines and placed them all into one bamboo tube'; '合诸药' is a Classical Chinese idiom for 'combine various medicines'.
  • 取以帽簮挿筒药中 - 'Took a hairpin from his hat and inserted it into the medicine in the tube'; '帽簮' is a hairpin used in ancient Chinese headwear.
  • 七日乃防取 - 'After seven days, it was removed'; '防' here is likely a variant of '方' (then, after) or possibly a typo.
  • 取簮以画酒中断饮一邉尽一邉尚满 - 'Took the hairpin and drew a line across the cup, one side drank up, the other remained full'; this is a metaphorical or symbolic act.
  • 北斗七星当化为人明 - 'The seven stars of the Big Dipper will transform into human beings'; this is a cosmological or astrological statement.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 管辂 - Guan Lu, a famous diviner and astrologer from the Three Kingdoms period (Wei dynasty), known for his supernatural abilities.
  • 郭恩 - Guo En, a layperson who visited Guan Lu and had a supernatural experience.
  • 隋炀帝 - Emperor Yang of the Sui dynasty, known for his ambitious projects and eventual downfall.
  • 秘书少监 - An official title in the Sui dynasty, indicating a low-ranking but important official in the imperial court.

  • Divination and supernatural prediction are central to the stories of Guan Lu and Li Chunfeng.
  • The story of '筹禅师' involves a magical wine that divides into two parts when drawn across with a hairpin.
  • Li Chunfeng’s prediction of a solar eclipse reflects Confucian and Daoist values of loyalty and truth.
  • Feng Shui and geomancy are implied in the context of Guan Lu’s ability to predict events based on omens and signs.

  • 即以一符置所见处 - '即以一符' means 'immediately with a talisman', and '置所见处' means 'placed it at the place where it was seen'.
  • 明日兽踣符下 - '明日' means 'the next day', '兽踣' means 'the beast fell', and '符下' means 'under the talisman'.
  • 我本以符却之使其不来岂知不能自脱既以害物安用术为 - '我本以符却之' means 'I originally used the talisman to repel it', '使其不来' means 'to make it not come', '岂知不能自脱' means 'how could I know it couldn't free itself', '既以害物' means 'it has already harmed things', and '安用术为' means 'how can I use magic for it?'.
  • 取符本焚之 - '取符本' means 'took the original talisman', and '焚之' means 'burned it'.
  • 自此絶不复留意 - '自此' means 'from this point on', '絶' means 'cut off', and '不复留意' means 'no longer paid attention'.
  • 有欧阳平者行业亦高又兄事蒋于田君即隣入室 - '有欧阳平者' means 'there was a person named Ouyang Ping', '行业亦高' means 'his industry was also high', '又兄事蒋' means 'and treated Jiang as a brother', '于田君' means 'to Lord Tian', '即隣入室' means 'immediately entered the room'.
  • 平一夕梦三金炉自天而下若有所召 - '平一夕' means 'Ping one night', '梦三金炉' means 'dreamed of three golden furnaces', '自天而下' means 'came down from the sky', and '若有所召' means 'as if called'.
  • 既寤潜告人曰二先生不乆去矣我继之 - '既寤' means 'after waking up', '潜告人' means 'secretly told someone', '曰' means 'said', '二先生不乆去矣' means 'the two masters will not be staying for long', and '我继之' means 'I will follow them'.
  • 俄而田蜕去蒋次之平亦游桐栢山 - '俄而' means 'soon', '田蜕去' means 'Lord Tian left', '蒋次之' means 'Jiang followed', and '平亦游桐栢山' means 'Ping also traveled to Tongbi Mountain'.
  • 陈寡言徐灵府冯云翼三人皆田之弟子衡山周混沌蒋之门人陈徐在东南品第比田蒋而冯在欧阳之列 - '陈寡言徐灵府冯云翼三人' means 'Chen Guanyan, Xu Lingfu, and Feng Yunyi, three people', '皆田之弟子' means 'were all disciples of Lord Tian', '衡山周混沌' means 'Hengshan Zhou Hun', '蒋之门人' means 'disciples of Jiang', '陈徐在东南品第比田蒋' means 'Chen and Xu in the southeast rank compared to Lord Tian and Jiang', and '而冯在欧阳之列' means 'and Feng is in the category of Ouyang'.
  • 周自幼入道利法清严今为南岳首冠 - '周自幼入道' means 'Zhou entered the Dao since childhood', '利法清严' means 'strict and clear methods', and '今为南岳首冠' means 'now is the chief of Nan Yue'.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 伐蜀回军在道而有邺都之变 - This clause combines two events: the return from the campaign against Shu and the rebellion in the capital.
  • 命内臣张汉賔赍急诏所在催魏王归阙 - The sentence structure implies that Zhang Hanbin was ordered to carry an urgent edict to urge Prince Wei to return to the capital.
  • 比事梁与汉賔熟密 - This phrase indicates a personal connection between Huang Chengzhen and Zhang Hanbin.
  • 三陈此术如不允行则止 - This conditional statement means that if the emperor does not agree to implement the secret method, Huang Chengzhen should stop.

  • 木坚毛柔是何别也 - How is the hardness of wood and the softness of fur different?
  • 以阳燧火山隂柘木之 - Using a sun mirror to heat a mountain of firewood and a yin zhe tree.
  • 木皮改常试之果验 - The wood's surface changed, and the test proved successful.
  • 明年冬扶南大舶从西天竺国来卖碧玻璃镜 - The following winter, a large ship from Funan arrived from the Western Kingdom of India to sell blue glass mirrors.
  • 面广一尺五寸重四十斤 - With a diameter of fifteen inches and weighing forty pounds.
  • 内外皎洁置五色物于其上向明视之不见其质 - The inside and outside are clear and bright; when five-colored objects are placed on it and viewed towards the light, their substance cannot be seen.
  • 问其价约钱百万贯文帝令有司算之倾府库偿之不足 - When asked about its price, it was said to be around a million strings of cash; Emperor Wen ordered the officials to calculate it, and even the entire treasury was insufficient to pay for it.
  • 其商人言此色界天王有福乐事天澍大雨众寳如山纳之山藏取之难得以大兽肉投之藏中肉烂黏寳一鸟衘出即此寳焉 - The merchant said that this blue glass mirror is a treasure of the Celestial King, who has great fortune and happiness. When the heavens rain down, treasures pile up like mountains, and they are stored in mountain caves. It is difficult to obtain these treasures, but if you throw the meat of a large animal into the cave, the meat will rot and stick to the treasures. A bird will then pick out the treasure.
  • 举国不识无敢酬其价者以示杰公公曰上界之寳信矣 - The entire country does not recognize it, and no one dares to pay its price. When shown to Master Jie, he said, 'It is indeed a treasure of the upper realms.'
  • 昔波罗尼斯国王有大福得获二寳镜镜光所照大者三十里小者十里至孙福尽天火烧宫大镜光明能御灾火不至焚小镜光微为火所 - In the past, the King of Pataliputra had great fortune and obtained two treasure mirrors. The light of the larger mirror could illuminate thirty miles, and the smaller one could illuminate ten miles. When the king's great-grandson's fortune ended, a fire burned down the palace. The larger mirror's light could prevent the disaster from spreading, but the smaller mirror's light was weak and was destroyed by the fire.

  • 害雖光彩昧暗尚能辟諸毒物方圆百歩蓋此鏡也 - Idiomatic expression describing the protective power of the mirror, stating that even when the mirror's light is dim, it can still ward off poisonous creatures within a hundred paces.
  • 時王賣得金二千余斤遂入商人之手 - Idiomatic expression describing the sequence of events: the king sold the mirror and received gold, which then passed into the hands of merchants.
  • 後王福薄失其大寳奪此鏡卻入王宮 - Idiomatic expression describing the cause-and-effect relationship: a later king, due to his thin fortune, lost the mirror, which was then taken back into the palace.
  • 此王十世孫失道國人謀害之 - Idiomatic expression '失道' meaning 'to lose one's way' or 'to go astray,' describing how the tenth-generation descendant of the king lost his way, and the people plotted to harm him.
  • 此鏡又出當是大臣所得其應入於商賈其價千金傾竭府庫不足也 - Idiomatic expression '傾竭府庫' meaning 'to exhaust the treasury,' describing how the mirror reappeared, likely in the hands of a minister, and its value was so great that even the entire treasury could not afford it.
  • 胡客逡巡未對俄而其國遣使追訪至梁云其鏡為盜所竊果如其言 - Idiomatic expression '俄而' meaning 'soon after,' and '果如其言' meaning 'as he had said,' describing how the merchants hesitated to respond, but soon after, an envoy from their country arrived in Liang, claiming that the mirror had been stolen.
  • 後有魏使頻至亦言黑貂白兎鴨馬女國往往入京梁朝卿士始信傑公周游六合出入百代言不虛說皆為美談故其多聞強識博物辯惑雖仲尼之詳大骨子產 - Idiomatic expression '周游六合' meaning 'to travel throughout the world,' and '出入百代言不虛說' meaning 'his words about things he has seen and heard are not false,' describing how envoys from Wei also arrived frequently, saying that black sables, white rabbits, duck horses, and women from various countries often entered the capital, leading the scholars of Liang to believe Jie Gong's stories.

  • 生有五子 - Literally 'gave birth to five sons,' but in this context, it likely refers to having five sons.
  • 僔倜俭位倚僔为考功员外俭为侍御史位为太常丞季子倚最贤 - Complex sentence structure listing the positions held by the sons, with '季子倚最贤' indicating that the youngest son, Yi, was the most virtuous.
  • 其姻媾皆天下族望 - Means 'his marriages were all to families of high standing,' emphasizing the prestigious nature of his family connections.

  • 终上疏曰 - Means 'finally submitted a memorial saying,' indicating the submission of a formal document.

  • 筋骸俱敝 - Means 'both muscles and bones are worn out,' describing extreme physical exhaustion.
  • 弥留沈困 - Means 'lying critically ill and deeply fatigued,' indicating a severe and prolonged illness.

  • 顾无成效 - Means 'but without any accomplishments,' expressing regret over a lack of significant achievements.

  • 永辞圣代 - Means 'to bid farewell to the holy era forever,' indicating the end of one's life and service.
  • 无任感恋之至 - Means 'with the utmost sense of gratitude and attachment,' expressing deep emotion.

  • 出雄藩垣 - Means 'to serve as a powerful governor in a frontier region,' indicating a high-ranking administrative position.
  • 入赞缉熈 - Means 'to assist in maintaining peace and harmony,' indicating a role in internal affairs.
  • 升平二纪 - Means 'two decades of peace and prosperity,' indicating a long period of stability.
  • 实卿是赖 - Means 'indeed, you are my reliance,' expressing dependence on the person.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 厯三公钟漏并歇筋骸俱敝弥留沈困殆溘尽 must be read as a single catalogue of the person's career and condition.
  • 顾无成效上答休明空负深恩永辞圣代无任感恋之至谨奉表称谢以闻 states the person's feelings and actions in response to their condition.
  • 卿以俊德作余元辅出雄藩垣入赞缉熈升平二纪实卿是赖 praises the person's qualities and role.
  • 比因疾累日谓痊除岂遽沈顿良深悯黙今遣骠骑大军高力士就第省其勉加针灸为余自爱 describes the ruler's actions and advice.
  • 防冀无妄期丁有喜其夕卒 indicates the time of death and the ruler's hope.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 业八体书生 - A title or self-identification of the scholar, indicating his expertise in calligraphy; '八体书' refers to eight traditional styles of Chinese writing.
  • 窃见古昔兴亡明主贤臣之事 - '窃见' is a humble expression meaning 'I have seen'; the phrase refers to the rise and fall of dynasties, wise rulers, and virtuous ministers.
  • 临一事即恣心狥 - '恣心' means 'to act according to one’s own will' or 'to be unrestrained in mind'; '狥' is an archaic term meaning 'to follow' or 'to yield to.' The phrase suggests acting without restraint or according to personal desires.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李林甫 - A powerful and influential chancellor during the Tang Dynasty (7th–8th century), known for his political cunning and suppression of rivals.
  • 布衣 - A term for a commoner or unranked person, often used to describe someone of low social status seeking an audience with a high official.
  • 阍吏 - A gatekeeper or guard at the entrance of a high-ranking official’s residence.
  • 宾馆 - A guest house or inn, where the scholar was received by Li Linfu.
  • 药石之言 - A metaphor for advice or admonition, derived from the idea that medicine (药) and stones (石) can cure illness — hence, 'words that can heal or cure.'

Proper Nouns

  • 李林甫 - Historical figure; keep as is.
  • 管子文 - Likely a fictional or historical figure in the text; keep as is.
  • 相国 - A high-ranking title for a chancellor, equivalent to prime minister; consistent translation as 'Chancellor' or 'Prime Minister.'
  • 布衣 - Term for a commoner; consistent translation as 'Commoner' or 'unranked person.'
  • 阍吏 - Term for a gatekeeper; consistent translation as 'Gatekeeper.'
  • 宾馆 - Term for a guest house; consistent translation as 'Guest house.'
  • 药石之言 - Metaphorical term for advice; consistent translation as 'Words of admonition' or 'words of healing.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 意如此则虽日纳献言之士亦无益也 - If it is like this, even if you daily receive those who offer advice, it will be of no benefit.
  • 君但一言教仆仆当书绅而永为箴诫 - If you just speak one word of instruction, I will write it on my sash and always take it as a warning.
  • 君闻美言必喜闻恶言必怒 - You hear good words and are sure to be pleased; you hear bad words and are sure to be angry.
  • 美言狥而损恶言直而益 - Good words are superficial and diminish; bad words are direct and beneficial.
  • 君当悉察之容我之言勿复加怒 - You should thoroughly consider my words and do not add anger to them.
  • 林甫不觉膝席而听 - Li Linfu did not realize he had knelt on his mat to listen.
  • 君为相相天子也相天子安宗社保国也 - You are the chancellor, serving the emperor. Serving the emperor means ensuring the safety of the ancestral temple and the nation.
  • 宗社安万国宁则天子无事天子无事则君之无事 - If the ancestral temple is safe and all the states are at peace, the emperor will have no worries; if the emperor has no worries, you will have no worries.
  • 设或天下有一人失所即罪在天子罪在天子焉用君相 - If there is even one person in the world who is out of place, then the fault lies with the emperor; if the fault lies with the emperor, what use is there for a chancellor?
  • 为相之道不必独任天下事当举文治天下之民举武定天下之乱 - The way of being a chancellor does not require you to handle all matters alone; you should promote culture to govern the people and use military force to resolve chaos.
  • 用仁人抚疲瘵用义士和鬭战自修节俭以讽上以化下自守忠贞以事主以律 - Use benevolent people to comfort the weak and sick; use righteous people to harmonize conflicts. Cultivate frugality to advise the ruler and transform the people; maintain loyalty and integrity to serve the ruler and set an example.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 林甫 - Li Linfu, a prominent chancellor during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 书绅 - A traditional practice where important words or phrases were written on a sash worn around the waist, symbolizing a commitment to remember and follow them.
  • 相天子 - The role of the chancellor in serving the emperor, a high-ranking official in the imperial court.
  • 宗社 - Refers to the ancestral temple, a symbol of the state and the emperor's lineage.
  • 疲瘵 - Refers to the weak and sick, indicating those in need of care and support.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 人固不暇躬勤庶政也 - Classical expression meaning 'people naturally do not have the time to personally attend to all matters of governance.'
  • 庶政得人即治 - Means 'if all matters of governance are entrusted to capable people, then the state will be well-governed.'
  • 茍不得人虽才如伊吕亦不治 - Concessive clause meaning 'if one cannot find capable people, even if they are as talented as Yi Yin and Lü Wang, the state will not be well-governed.'
  • 噫相国慎之 - Exclamation urging caution, where '噫' is an interjection similar to 'ah' or 'oh,' and '相国' refers to a high-ranking official or prime minister.
  • 林甫听之骇然遽起拜谢之 - Describes a reaction of surprise and immediate reverence, where '骇然' means 'in astonishment,' '遽起' means 'rise up quickly,' and '拜谢' means 'bow and thank.'
  • 公知斯运之通塞邪 - Rhetorical question asking whether the official understands the ups and downs of fortune, where '通塞' refers to prosperity and adversity.
  • 治生乱乱生治 - Philosophical statement about the cyclical nature of order and chaos, where '治' means 'order' and '乱' means 'chaos.'
  • 我国家自革隋乱而治至扵今日乱生矣 - Statement about the historical transition from disorder to order and back to disorder, where '革隋乱' refers to the overthrow of the Sui dynasty's chaos, and '乱生矣' means 'disorder has arisen again.'
  • 君其记之 - Imperative meaning 'you must remember this.'
  • 林甫又拜谢至曙欲闻扵上縻以一爵禄 - Describes the official's reaction and subsequent actions, where '至曙' means 'until dawn,' '欲闻扵上' means 'want to report to the emperor,' and '縻以一爵禄' means 'to be bound by a title and salary.'
  • 令左右濳守之坚求退曰我本秪欲逹一言扵公今得竭愚悃而又辱见纳又何用阻野人之归也 - Complex sentence describing the official's request to leave, where '令左右濳守之' means 'order his attendants to secretly guard him,' '坚求退' means 'firmly request to leave,' '我本秪欲逹一言扵公' means 'I only wanted to convey one word to you,' '今得竭愚悃' means 'now I have fully expressed my sincere advice,' and '又何用阻野人之归也' means 'why should I be prevented from returning to the countryside?'
  • 林甫坚留之不得遂去 - Describes the official's persistence in keeping the person, but ultimately failing, where '坚留之' means 'firmly keep him,' '不得' means 'unable to,' and '遂去' means 'finally left.'
  • 林甫令人暗逐之生至南山中一石洞其人寻亦入石洞遽不见生唯有故旧大笔一其人携以白林甫 - Describes the mysterious disappearance of the person, where '暗逐之' means 'secretly follow him,' '至南山中一石洞' means 'to a cave in the southern mountains,' '寻亦入石洞' means 'soon entered the cave,' '遽不见生' means 'suddenly disappeared,' '唯有故旧大笔一' means 'only left behind an old brush,' and '携以白林甫' means 'took it to show to Lin Fu.'
  • 林甫以其笔置于书阁焚香拜祝其夕笔忽化为一五色禽飞去不知所之 - Describes the miraculous transformation of the brush, where '置于书阁' means 'placed it in the book cabinet,' '焚香拜祝' means 'burn incense and pray,' '其夕' means 'that night,' '笔忽化为一五色禽' means 'the brush suddenly transformed into a five-colored bird,' and '飞去不知所之' means 'flew away, its destination unknown.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 伊吕 - Refers to Yi Yin and Lü Wang, two legendary ministers of ancient China known for their wisdom and virtue.
  • 林甫 - Refers to Li Linfu, a prominent and controversial official during the Tang Dynasty, known for his political influence and cunning.
  • 革隋乱 - Refers to the overthrow of the Sui dynasty, which was marked by political instability and social unrest.

Proper Nouns

  • 伊吕 - Yi Yin and Lü Wang.
  • 林甫 - Li Linfu.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 唐宁王傅 - Refers to an official position in the Tang Dynasty, specifically a tutor or advisor to the Prince of Ning.
  • 能行大节 - Means 'able to perform great deeds' or 'capable of upholding great principles.'
  • 犯顔悟主 - A classical expression meaning 'to boldly speak out to the emperor to enlighten him.'
  • 虽死不避 - Means 'even if it means death, he does not avoid it.'

  • 眼看使扵蠮蠛国未知死所何怒我焉 - A cryptic phrase meaning 'you will see yourself sent to the land of the Yimu, where you won't know where you'll die, so why are you angry at me?'
  • 嘉祚思其言不能解异之明复至 - Means 'Yuan Jiaxiao pondered his words but could not understand them, and the next day returned again.'

  • 嘉辞以不才 - Means 'Jia refused, saying he was unworthy.'

  • 闻宰相欲令使远国信乎 - Means 'Hearing that the prime minister wanted to send an envoy to a distant country, is that true?'
  • 嘉祚下马拜之 - Means 'Jia dismounted and bowed to him.'
  • 异人曰公无忧也且止不行其二相头已悬枪刃矣焉能怒公 - Means 'The strange man said, 'You have no worries. Do not go. The heads of the two prime ministers are already hanging on spears. How could they be angry with you?''
  • 果如异人言矣 - Means 'Indeed, as the strange man said.'
  • 其蠮蠛国在大秦国西数千里自古未尝通 - Means 'The country of Yemei is several thousand miles west of the Great Qin, and has never been connected since ancient times.'
  • 二相死嘉祚竟不去 - Means 'After the two prime ministers died, Jia still did not leave.'
  • 郑防工诗嗜酒性甚间放 - Means 'Zheng Fang was skilled in poetry, fond of wine, and very unrestrained in his nature.'
  • 又以其不事事故特置广文馆命防为博士 - Means 'Also, because he did not concern himself with official duties, he specially established the Guangwen Academy and appointed Fang as a Doctor.'
  • 名籍甚着门庭车马无非才俊 - Means 'His reputation was very prominent, and his courtyard was full of talented and distinguished people.'
  • 有郑相如者沧州人应进士举入京闻防重名以宗姓因谒防 - Means 'There was a man named Zheng Xiangru from Cangzhou who was taking the imperial examinations and came to the capital, hearing of Fang's great reputation, and because they were of the same clan, he visited Fang.'
  • 因之叙叔侄见其潦倒未甚敬之后数日谒防独与坐问其艺业 - Means 'He then arranged to meet as uncle and nephew, but seeing his poverty, he was not very respectful. Several days later, he visited Fang alone and sat with him, asking about his literary and scholarly achievements.'
  • 相如笑谓防曰叔未知相如应以凡人遇然人未易知既见问敢不尽其词 - Means 'Xiangru smiled and said to Fang, 'Uncle, you do not know that I should be treated as an ordinary person. People are not easy to understand. Now that you have asked, how dare I not tell you everything?''
  • 相如若在孔门当处四科犹居游夏之右若叔在孔门不得列为四科 - Means 'If I were in Confucius' school, I would be in one of the four categories and would be above You and Xia. If you were in Confucius' school, you would not be listed among the four categories.'
  • 今生不遇时而应此常调但销声晦迹而已 - Means 'Now, not meeting the times, I should only be silent and obscure myself.'
  • 防闻之甚惊请穷其说 - Means 'Fang was greatly surprised and asked for a detailed explanation.'
  • 相如曰孔子称其或继周者虽百世可知之也今相如亦知之然国家至开元三十年当改年号后十五年当有 - Means 'Xiangru said, 'Confucius said that if someone were to follow the Zhou dynasty, even a hundred generations later, it would be known. Now, I also know that. The country will change its era name in the thirtieth year of Kaiyuan, and fifteen years later, there will be...''
